reforms.description: Estonia is considering introducing tax incentives for R&D to enhance support for innovation and globalisation activities, which are currently primarily funded through EU structural funds and grants.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Tax incentives for R&D are expected to stimulate private sector investment in innovation, thereby enhancing productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Consideration should be given to introducing tax incentives for R&D.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of tax incentives for R&D represents a significant shift in the funding approach for innovation, aiming to enhance the effectiveness of existing support mechanisms.
reforms.source_quote: In order to avoid a not very promising 'picking winners' strategy, more consideration should be given to introducing tax incentives for R&D.

reforms.description: The Estonian authorities are exploring the development of a local capital venture market to provide adequate seed capital for small firms, which is crucial for fostering innovation and entrepreneurship.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Creating a local capital venture market is expected to enhance access to financing for startups, thereby promoting innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Adequate seed capital should be made available by developing a local capital venture market.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative to develop a local capital venture market is significant for enhancing the startup ecosystem, which is vital for innovation-driven growth.
reforms.source_quote: Adequate seed capital should be made available by developing a local capital venture market.

reforms.description: Estonia accelerated the use of EU structural funds to support investment and employment during the recession, increasing the total amount of EU grants from 2.2% of GDP in 2008 to 5.1% of GDP in 2009.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The rapid use of EU funds helped mitigate the negative impacts of fiscal tightening by boosting investment and supporting startups, which can enhance economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The total amount of EU grants increased significantly during the recession.
reforms.importance_rationale: The acceleration of EU fund utilization represents a significant response to economic challenges, enhancing support for startups and investment during a critical period.
reforms.source_quote: The total amount of the EU grants increased from 2.2% of GDP in 2008 to 5.1% of GDP in 2009.

reforms.description: The Debt Restructuring and Debt Protection Act introduces a new individual debt restructuring procedure, allowing debtors to negotiate their debt obligations with creditor approval, thereby providing an alternative to bankruptcy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing a more flexible and less costly way to resolve bad debts, the reform can help maintain viable loan contracts and support economic recovery.
reforms.status_evidence: The Debt Restructuring and Debt Protection Act was legislated in November 2010.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform significantly alters the legal framework for dealing with non-performing loans, providing a new mechanism for debt resolution that can enhance economic stability.
reforms.source_quote: The Debt Restructuring and Debt Protection Act makes a number of important changes to the way that NPLs could be dealt with.

reforms.description: Estonia has implemented a pilot voucher scheme allowing the unemployed to choose relevant training programs, with funding increased for 2011 to enhance its scope and effectiveness in addressing skills mismatches in the labor market.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The voucher scheme increases the relevance of training for the unemployed, thereby improving their employability and addressing skills mismatches in the labor market.
reforms.status_evidence: The scheme is expected to continue in 2011 with increased funding and expanded scope.
reforms.importance_rationale: The voucher scheme represents a significant enhancement of existing training measures, targeting skills mismatches and improving labor market outcomes for the unemployed.
reforms.source_quote: Another useful innovation was the adoption from October 2009 of a pilot voucher scheme, which allows the unemployed, together with a PES consultant, to choose a relevant training scheme up to a cost of EUR1000.

reforms.description: Estonia has introduced a business start-up scheme that provides unemployed individuals with a start-up grant, business training, and ongoing mentor support to encourage entrepreneurship.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The scheme aims to reduce unemployment by fostering entrepreneurship, which can lead to job creation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The scheme is currently operational and providing support to unemployed individuals.
reforms.importance_rationale: The start-up scheme represents a significant effort to address unemployment through entrepreneurship, although it is not a comprehensive overhaul of the innovation ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: The range of activation measures has also been expanded by offering a business start-up scheme.

reforms.description: Estonia is developing micro-financing based on start-up loans to improve access to financing for local SMEs, which is crucial for fostering innovation and entrepreneurship.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing access to financing for SMEs can stimulate innovation and business growth, contributing positively to economic development.
reforms.status_evidence: The development of micro-financing based on start-up loans is in operation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant step towards improving financial access for SMEs, which is essential for fostering innovation and entrepreneurship in the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The development of micro-financing based on start-up loans is in operation.

reforms.description: Estonia has initiated a project to introduce a national database of medical documents, which includes electronic health records, digital registrations, and digital imaging databases, aimed at improving healthcare efficiency.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The project enhances the efficiency of healthcare services, potentially reducing costs and improving patient outcomes through better data management.
reforms.status_evidence: The project finally got under way in 2008 building three databases.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of a national medical database represents a significant step towards digitalisation in healthcare, improving service delivery and data management.
reforms.source_quote: A project for introducing a national database of medical documents has been on the agenda since 2003 and with some delay it finally got under way in 2008 building three databases.

reforms.description: The introduction of an electronic e-Prescription system in Estonia aims to enhance the monitoring of drug consumption and prescription patterns, improving healthcare efficiency.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The e-Prescription system is expected to streamline the prescription process, reduce errors, and enhance data collection on drug usage, leading to better healthcare outcomes.
reforms.status_evidence: The electronic e-Prescription system launched in 2010.
reforms.importance_rationale: The e-Prescription system represents a significant advancement in healthcare technology, improving the efficiency and safety of the prescription process.
reforms.source_quote: The latest stage of this e-health approach is an electronic e-Prescription system launched in 2010.

reforms.description: Enterprise Estonia (EAS) was established to promote business and regional policy, providing financial assistance, advisory services, and training for entrepreneurs, research establishments, and the public sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By supporting entrepreneurship and innovation, EAS enhances the capacity of firms to grow and compete, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: EAS has been operational since its establishment in 2000.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of EAS represents a significant institutional change aimed at fostering entrepreneurship and innovation, which is crucial for the economic development of Estonia.
reforms.source_quote: Enterprise Estonia (EAS) was established in 2000 and promotes business and regional policy in Estonia.

reforms.description: The Estonian National Development Plan includes measures aimed at financing R&D and innovation, strengthening innovation systems, and increasing awareness about innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By financing R&D and innovation, the plan aims to enhance the technological capabilities of firms, leading to improved productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The measures are part of the Estonian National Development Plan.
reforms.importance_rationale: The measures represent a significant commitment to enhancing R&D and innovation, although they are part of a broader strategic framework.
reforms.source_quote: EAS is the implementing body for the technology and innovation measures of the Estonian National Development Plan.

reforms.description: The Estonian government aims to support entrepreneurs in various phases of internationalisation by reducing one-off sunk costs and risks associated with foreign operations, including the establishment of support units and a counselling network for potential exporters.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitating internationalisation can enhance market access and competitiveness for Estonian firms, leading to increased productivity and growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government aims to support entrepreneurs in the different phases of internationalisation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant effort to enhance the international competitiveness of Estonian firms, particularly small enterprises, by providing targeted support.
reforms.source_quote: The government aims to support entrepreneurs in the different phases of internationalisation, through the reduction of one-off sunk costs and reducing the risks of foreign operations.

reforms.description: The Estonian Development Fund was established to nurture early-stage seed and venture capital investments into technology-based start-ups and to develop effective long-term policies for technological companies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing capital to technology-based start-ups, the fund aims to stimulate innovation and entrepreneurship, which can lead to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Estonian Development Fund became operational in 2007.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of the Estonian Development Fund represents a major reform in the funding landscape for start-ups, aiming to enhance the innovation ecosystem in Estonia.
reforms.source_quote: The Estonian Development Fund became operational in 2007, charged with nurturing early stage seed/venture capital investments into technology based start-ups.

reforms.description: Estonia aims to raise its R&D spending from 1.5% of GDP in 2008 to 3% by 2014, with a focus on increasing private sector contributions to R&D funding.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D funding is expected to enhance productivity and innovation, contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The target for R&D spending was set as part of the national strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in R&D funding, aiming to shift the balance towards private sector contributions and enhance overall innovation capacity.
reforms.source_quote: Knowledge-based Estonia’ set a target of raising spending on research and development from 1½ per cent of GDP in 2008 to just under 2% by 2010 and 3% by 2014.

reforms.description: The Estonian government is modernising R&D infrastructure and increasing pay for researchers to enhance the quality and quantity of R&D personnel.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving R&D infrastructure and researcher compensation is expected to boost research output and innovation capacity.
reforms.status_evidence: Co-ordinated large-scale investments are being carried out over the 2008-15 period.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses critical deficiencies in the R&D framework, aiming for a comprehensive upgrade of research capabilities.
reforms.source_quote: Measures are being taken to enhance the quality and numbers of people working in research and development through increased pay and the modernisation of R&D infrastructure.

reforms.description: Estonia aims to enhance its education system to better align with the needs of the knowledge society, focusing on increasing the supply of scientific graduates and engineers to address productivity gaps.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing the number of skilled graduates in science and engineering, the reform is expected to enhance productivity and innovation capacity in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The need for educational reform to meet the demands of the knowledge society has been highlighted.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses fundamental issues in the education system that are critical for the long-term economic development and competitiveness of Estonia.
reforms.source_quote: It will be important to provide incentives for a sufficient supply of new-generation researchers and engineers and create attractive possibilities for their employment.

reforms.description: Estonia plans to improve vocational education by modernizing the study environment and enhancing curriculum development to attract more students and reduce drop-out rates.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing vocational education, the reform aims to equip the workforce with necessary skills, thereby improving employability and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The policy has been to expand vocational education and a number of changes have been made to the system.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is crucial for addressing the skills gap in the labor market and ensuring that vocational education meets the needs of the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The policy has been to expand vocational education and, following the Development Plan for the Estonian Vocational Education and Training System 2005-08, a number of changes have been made.
